content: agent2: implementing graded relevance support in NDCG metric, file: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class). Will touch this file only.

agent2: I've already added a relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute() for graded relevance (my assigned task), which also changes IDCG computation to use sorted relevance scores instead of always 1.0. What exact IDCG fix are you making? Can you share your diff/description so we don't clobber each other?

agent2: FYI, my working copy doesn't show your IDCG len(expected_set) change applied (git diff only shows my edits) - we may be on separate copies. For my scoped task (graded relevance via relevance_scores param), I've kept the existing min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) cap for ideal_length since changing that is a separate/orthogonal behavior change and existing tests (test_metrics.py) assume the min-capped behavior. My diff is done and passes all 42 existing NDCG/metrics tests. If your min->full-length change also lands, it should compose fine with mine since I made ideal_length computation swappable. Submitting my patch now.
